description: Workout Plan Editing & Custom Exercise Management — users can add, remove, and swap exercises on today's plan, create custom exercises saved to their profile, and have all custom exercises feed into PR tracking.

userStories.title: Exercise Picker Component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable exercise picker widget that lets users search and select an exercise from the combined database + custom exercise list.
acceptanceCriteria: Picker is a bottom sheet modal
Shows all exercises from the exercise database + user's custom exercises
Search bar at the top filters by name in real time (case-insensitive)
Exercises grouped by category (Push / Pull / Legs / Cardio / Custom)
Each item shows: exercise name, primary muscle group(s), equipment tag
Tap an exercise to select it — closes picker and returns the selected exercise
Picker accepts an optional excludeIds list to hide already-added exercises
Unit tests: search filtering, category grouping, exclusion list
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Custom Exercise Service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service layer to manage CRUD operations for custom exercises in Firestore.
acceptanceCriteria: CustomExerciseService class created with methods: getCustomExercises(userId), createCustomExercise(userId, exercise), updateCustomExercise(userId, exerciseId, updates), deleteCustomExercise(userId, exerciseId)
getCustomExercises returns a Stream that merges database exercises and custom exercises
Name uniqueness validated at service level — throws if duplicate name (case-insensitive)
Custom exercises stored at Firestore path: users/{userId}/custom_exercises/{exerciseId}
CustomExercise model includes: id, name, primaryMuscles, secondaryMuscles, equipment, category, notes, isCustom: true, createdAt
Unit tests for all methods: create, read, update, delete, duplicate name check
Golden path e2e test: create custom exercise → appears in getCustomExercises stream
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Workout Plan Edit State Management
userStories.description: As a developer, I need state management for today's plan edits so changes are tracked cleanly without corrupting recommendation data.
acceptanceCriteria: TodayWorkoutNotifier (Riverpod) manages mutable state of today's plan
State tracks original recommended exercises plus user additions, removals, and swaps separately
Plan edits persisted to the WorkoutLog in Firestore under an edits sub-field: { added: [], removed: [], swapped: [] }
Recommendation engine (RecommendationService, FatigueService) never reads the edits field — continues to read only from workout_logs exercise sets and muscle_fatigue
On workout completion, fatigue is calculated from actually logged exercises regardless of whether they were recommended, added, or swapped
Unit tests: add exercise to state, remove exercise from state, swap exercise, completion calculates from logged exercises only
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Exercise to Today's Plan
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add an exercise to today's workout so I can include something the app didn't suggest.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Add Exercise' button visible on the Today screen below the exercise list
Tapping it opens the Exercise Picker (US-021)
Selected exercise is appended to the bottom of today's plan
Added exercise shows a visual 'Added by you' tag to distinguish it from recommended ones
Added exercise supports full logging (sets/reps/weight/RPE) via the existing ExerciseLoggingModal
Addition persisted to the workout log in Firestore with field isCustomAdded: true
Already-added exercises are excluded from the picker so duplicates cannot be added
No cap on the number of exercises a user can add to a single workout
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Remove Exercise from Today's Plan
userStories.description: As a user, I want to remove an exercise from today's plan if I don't want to do it.
acceptanceCriteria: Each exercise card on the Today screen supports swipe-left or long-press to reveal a 'Remove' option
No confirmation required for exercises not yet logged — removal is immediate
If the exercise has already been logged (marked complete), a confirmation dialog appears: 'This exercise has been logged. Remove it and its log?'
Removed exercise disappears from the list immediately
Removal persisted to Firestore
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Undo Remove Snackbar
userStories.description: As a user, I want a quick undo option after removing an exercise so I don't lose it by accident.
acceptanceCriteria: After removing an exercise, a snackbar appears with message '[Exercise Name] removed. Undo'
Tapping 'Undo' re-adds the exercise to its original position in the list
Snackbar dismisses after 5 seconds
Undo is not available if user navigates away before the snackbar dismisses
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Swap Exercise on Today's Plan
userStories.description: As a user, I want to swap a suggested exercise for a different one so I can adjust for equipment availability or preference.
acceptanceCriteria: Each exercise card has a swap icon (↔) or long-press → 'Swap' option
Tapping swap opens the Exercise Picker (US-021) pre-filtered to the same primary muscle group(s) as the exercise being swapped
User can clear the muscle filter to see all exercises
Selected replacement takes the position of the original in the list
Replacement exercise pre-fills set/rep/weight values from the original exercise's suggestions; all fields remain editable
Swapped exercise shows a 'Swapped' visual tag
Swap persisted to Firestore with fields isSwapped: true and replacedExerciseId: <original id>
If the original exercise had already been logged, that log data is cleared and user is warned before proceeding
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Custom Exercise Creation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create my own exercise so I can track activities not in the database.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Create Custom Exercise' option visible inside the Exercise Picker as a button at the top or bottom of the list

Opens a form modal with fields: Name (required, max 50 chars), Primary muscle group(s) (required, multi-select), Secondary muscle group(s) (optional, multi-select), Equipment (optional, single-select), Category (required: Push / Pull / Legs / Cardio / Other), Notes (optional)
Name must be unique within the user's custom exercises — inline error shown if duplicate
After saving, the new exercise is automatically selected in the picker — closes both the form and picker and returns the new exercise
Custom exercises appear in the Exercise Picker under the 'Custom' category with a star icon indicator
Custom exercises are tracked in the PR system — personal records recorded and updated on the same basis as database exercises
Unit tests: validation for missing name, duplicate name, missing muscle group
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Manage Custom Exercises Library
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view, edit, and delete my custom exercises so I can keep my library tidy.
acceptanceCriteria: 'My Exercises' section accessible from Profile screen
Lists all user-created custom exercises showing name, muscle groups, and category
Tap an exercise to edit it — same form as creation, pre-filled with existing values
Swipe left or long-press → 'Delete' to remove a custom exercise
Deletion confirmation dialog: 'Delete [Name]? It will be removed from future selection.'
Deleted custom exercise removed from Firestore
If deleted exercise is currently on today's plan, it remains for the day flagged as 'Deleted' and cannot be added again
Empty state shown when no custom exercises exist: 'No custom exercises yet. Add one from the workout screen.'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
